When tissues of the apple are exposed to oxygen, an enzyme called POLYPHENOL OXIDASE (PPO) activates & begins converting phenolic compounds to quinones. These polymerize, like beads on a string, into ALLOMELANIN. Allomelanin acts like a blood clot, creating a barrier that blocks further damage.
